blocks.content: The room went still. The music faded. This was the part where it became real.
Pastor Gerald Hayes had officiated forty-seven weddings at Millhaven Baptist. He took his time with every one.

blocks.speaker: Pastor
blocks.content: We gather today in the presence of God and these witnesses to join Kit Jenkins and Daisy May Jones in marriage. Marriage is not a light thing. It is a covenant — a promise made here and carried out in every ordinary morning that follows.
Kit kept his eyes on Daisy's face. She was steady.

blocks.speaker: Pastor
blocks.content: Love is patient. Love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast, it is not proud. It does not dishonor others. It keeps no record of wrongs. It always protects, always trusts, always hopes, always perseveres. Love never fails.

blocks.speaker: Pastor
blocks.content: Who presents this woman to be married?
Mr. Jones's voice was quiet and even.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Jones
blocks.content: Her mother and I do. With great joy.
He found his wife in the front row. She was not pretending.

blocks.speaker: Pastor
blocks.content: Kit and Daisy, you have come here today to make a promise to each other before every person in this room who loves you. I am told you have both written words of your own, and we will get to those. But first — there are some things that need to be said.
